pattern: Every transaction is validated against the same two rules (account numbers in range, and sufficient funds for money leaving an account) before any balance is mutated, so a rejected transaction never has partial side effects.
hints: Store the balances so account numbers (1-indexed) map directly to them; validate an account number is in range before touching anything.

transfer, deposit, and withdraw all share the same validity shape: check every account number involved is in range, and for money LEAVING an account, check the balance covers it, before changing any state.
Compute whether the transaction is valid first, and only mutate balances if it is; a transfer that fails the balance check must leave both accounts completely untouched, not partially debited.

edge_inputs.description: statement example: a valid withdraw, a valid transfer, a valid deposit, a transfer that overdraws, and a withdraw on a nonexistent account
account number equal to n (the last valid account) succeeds; n + 1 is out of range and fails
withdrawing or transferring exactly the full balance succeeds and leaves exactly zero, one unit more fails
a transfer from an account to itself still requires sufficient balance and is otherwise a no-op on the net total
withdrawing or transferring zero money is always valid regardless of balance
a single-account bank rejects any transfer (both endpoints must be valid, and account 2 does not exist)
money at the constraint's upper bound deposited then withdrawn

stress.description: random mix of transfer/deposit/withdraw across a modest number of accounts, including frequent out-of-range account numbers and near-balance amounts
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = 30
    balances = [rng.randint(0, 1000) for _ in range(n)]
    ops = ['Bank']
    args = [[balances]]
    for _ in range(max(1, size)):
        c = rng.choice(['transfer', 'deposit', 'withdraw'])
        acct_choices = list(range(1, n + 1)) + [n + 1, n + 5]
        if c == 'transfer':
            a1 = rng.choice(acct_choices)
            a2 = rng.choice(acct_choices)
            money = rng.randint(0, 500)
            ops.append('transfer'); args.append([a1, a2, money])
        elif c == 'deposit':
            a = rng.choice(acct_choices)
            money = rng.randint(0, 500)
            ops.append('deposit'); args.append([a, money])
        else:
            a = rng.choice(acct_choices)
            money = rng.randint(0, 500)
            ops.append('withdraw'); args.append([a, money])
    return [ops, args]
stress.note: occasionally sampling account numbers just past n forces the out-of-range rejection path often
